title: Pyrrocoma linearis
taxonomy: Pyrrocoma linearis is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrrocoma, which is a group of flowering plants native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The species Pyrrocoma linearis is further classified as a type of shrub or subshrub. Within the Pyrrocoma genus, Pyrrocoma linearis is distinct due to its unique linear leaves and yellow flowers. Further classification may involve identifying it as part of a specific section or series within the Pyrrocoma genus.

intro: Pyrrocoma linearis, also known as Colorado rubberroot, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used by indigenous cultures for its medicinal properties, which include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and alleviating fever, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrocoma linearis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pyrrocoma linearis is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations and infections.
